content.content:
1	# Handoff — Laboratory implementation, continuing
2	
3	The implementation thread that started with the apparatus
4	skeleton has now landed three real engine pairs. What's left:
5	the archive engine + probes (blocked on the design click
6	c-momaa5o9), and a stack of unblocked-but-mechanical pieces —
7	codified smoke test, infra-spec retirement, documentation, and
8	the first real-world trial port.
9	
10	## Where we are
11	
12	Eight commits past the apparatus skeleton:
13	
14	  b306ab98  apparatus skeleton
15	  d6387a24  rig template + skeleton engines
16	  776db61f  staged phase orchestrators
17	  65cc30ab  manifest CLI (`nsg lab-trial-post`)
18	  3cc167c1  smoke-test confirmation (live trial in vibers)
19	  5b4b71b8  codex fixture engines (local-bare flow)
20	  f93266bc  guild fixture engines
21	  cc83efd2  cross-guild scenario engines
22	  62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of all three engine pairs
23	
24	Three engine pairs are real and live-verified against vibers:
25	
26	  - `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}`        (c-moma9y1k)
27	      → engines/codex-fixture.ts
28	      Local bare repo per trial; no GitHub. Auto-default
29	      codexName = `<slug>-<writId-tail-8>`.
30	
31	  - `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}`        (c-momaa03d)
32	      → engines/guild-fixture.ts
33	      `nsg init` → plugin install loop → duck-typed codex
34	      registration → guild.json deep-merge → file copies.
35	      Default guildPath = `<labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/`.
36	
37	  - `lab.commission-post-xguild`
38	    `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` (c-momaa1vt)
39	      → engines/scenario-xguild.ts
40	      commission-post inline-polls until terminal by default
41	      (waitForTerminal=true). wait engine standalone for future
42	      multi-step scenarios.
43	
44	Cross-cutting changes: phases.ts now injects `_trial:{slug,
45	writId, fixtureId}` into every grafted work-engine's givens —
46	general improvement, lights up trial-aware defaulting in all
47	fixture engines. Stub-engine console logging removed.
48	
49	123/123 unit tests passing across the package. Two trials
50	posted live against vibers, both completed cleanly (11 engines
51	each, ~14s wallclock). The daemon currently running has all
52	this code loaded.
53	
54	## What's left
55	
56	### Blocked on the archive design conversation (c-momaa5o9)
57	
58	Two engines depend on knowing where archived trial data lives.
59	The companion handoff at `.scratch/handoff-laboratory-archive-design.md`
60	walks through three options (A: lab-host books, B: sanctum
61	mirror, C: hybrid) and suggests a stress-test → pick → specify
62	flow. Until that's resolved, the following are blocked:
63	
64	  - **`lab.archive`** (c-momaa5o9)
65	      Final consolidation step. Yields should include archive-
66	      target identifier (writ id if A/C, filesystem path if B)
67	      so the trial writ's resolution carries forward where to
68	      find the archive. Once landed, the teardown engines'
69	      archive-presence safety check tightens from "context.upstream.archive
70	      defined" to "archived === true".
71	
72	  - **`lab.probe-stacks-dump` + `lab.probe-git-range`** (c-momaa3w7)
73	      Output shape depends on archive design. Stacks dump
74	      exports the test guild's books — JSON-per-table under (B),
75	      rows-into-lab-books under (A), or both under (C). Git-range
76	      captures commits between codex base and head SHAs (per-commit
77	      diffs + commits-manifest). Both probes read upstream paths
78	      from their context.upstream — explicit declared upstreams
79	      in their grafted entry.
80	
81	### Unblocked
82	
83	  - **Codified smoke test** (c-momaa75l)
84	      The live verifies in `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml`
85	      worked end-to-end; codify them as a recurring package
86	      test. Pattern: build a synthetic guild in tmp, post a
87	      trivial trial via the CLI, assert rig completes with
88	      every expected engine in the expected order, clean up.
89	      Look at spider's integration tests for precedent
90	      (`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider-test-fixture.ts`).
91	
92	  - **Retire the superseded infra spec** (c-momaa8mk)
93	      `experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md` is
94	      what the apparatus replaces. Decide: historical-archive
95	      vs delete-since-superseded. Conclude `c-molveg89` (the
96	      click that tracked the spec as a P3 prereq) with a pointer
97	      to the apparatus.
98	
99	  - **Documentation** (c-momaaa3t)
100	      `packages/laboratory/README.md` — flesh out manifest
101	      authoring, the rig flow, and the archive layout (per
102	      whatever the archive design picks). Apparatus doc under
103	      `/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/` describing the
104	      apparatus's place in the architecture. User-facing guide
105	      "evaluating prompts/plugins via lab trials" living in the
106	      sanctum since end-users consume sanctum-side docs.
107	
108	  - **First real-world trial** (c-momaab8y)
109	      Likely X016 orientation suppression — see the
110	      cost-optimization landscape (c-mok4nke6) for the
111	      candidate list. Author its manifest, run it, verify
112	      findings land where expected. The capstone — validates
113	      the apparatus on real research work and produces the
114	      first real findings via the new pipeline. Depends on
115	      archive being real.
116	
117	### Grit surfaced from live verify (no click yet)
118	
119	Two operational concerns emerged from posting trials against
120	vibers. Worth opening clicks for follow-up; not blocking:
121	
122	  - **Failed-trial orphan cleanup.** When `guild-setup` fails
123	    after `codex-setup` succeeds, the rig fails without running
124	    teardowns. The codex stays registered in the lab-host, the
125	    bare repo stays on disk. Two design directions: setup
126	    engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires
127	    on any rig terminal-state, OR teardowns run unconditionally
128	    on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream
129	    chain succeeded).
130	
131	  - **Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove`
132	    invoked from the CLI runs in a separate process, updates
133	    guild.json, but the running daemon's in-memory Scriptorium
134	    state isn't notified. Daemon's next guild.json write
135	    (during the next successful trial's teardown) serializes
136	    its in-memory state and reverts the orphans. Vibers
137	    currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes
138	    (`verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}`) that will
139	    only clear on daemon restart. Cosmetic for now (orphan bare
140	    repos don't run code), but means CLI-side orphan cleanup
141	    doesn't stick.
142	
143	## Implementation discipline
144	
145	Same as before; preserved here for continuity:
146	
147	  - **One commit per engine.** Each commit ships the engine +
148	    its tests + replaces the stub in `stubs.ts` + adds the real
149	    implementation file under `engines/`. Conclude the matching
150	    click in the same commit.
151	  - **Match framework conventions.** Each engine is an
152	    `EngineDesign` with `id`, `run(givens, context)`, and
153	    optional `retry`. Errors throw; the spider routes to engine
154	    failure.
155	  - **Givens carry the contract.** Document each engine's
156	    givens shape in a TS comment block at the top of the file.
157	    Manifest authors read those to know what to put in
158	    `ext.laboratory.config`.
159	  - **Live verification per engine.** After implementing,
160	    post a trial that exercises it and check the rig completes.
161	
162	## Pointers to read first
163	
164	  - `nsg click extract --id c-moma9llq` — full implementation
165	    click subtree.
166	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/phases.ts` — phase
167	    orchestrators that route into each engine; now also
168	    documents the `InjectedTrialContext` shape that every
169	    fixture engine sees.
170	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/codex-fixture.ts` — first
171	    real engine pair; pattern for fixture engines (validation,
172	    rollback, archive-presence safety check).
173	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/guild-fixture.ts` —
174	    pattern for engines that shell out to nsg, with sequential
175	    plugin-install loop.
176	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts` —
177	    pattern for engines that shell out + parse JSON +
178	    optionally poll.
179	  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/stubs.ts` — what's still
180	    a stub. The remaining `lab.archive`, `lab.probe-stacks-dump`,
181	    `lab.probe-git-range` are here.
182	  - `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` — verify manifests
183	    used in live testing; useful templates for the codified
184	    smoke test.
185	
186	## Live test environment
187	
188	Vibers is the lab-host guild for development. The daemon
189	loaded the latest code as of session cb9f1554. After plugin
190	code changes, restart vibers' daemon to pick up new engine
191	registrations. The CLI tool (`nsg lab-trial-post`) reads source
192	per-invocation (fresh node process), but the running spider
193	loaded its engines at startup.
194	
195	**Caveat:** vibers currently has three orphan codexes from

file.content:
# Handoff — Laboratory implementation, continuing

The implementation thread that started with the apparatus
skeleton has now landed three real engine pairs. What's left:
the archive engine + probes (blocked on the design click
c-momaa5o9), and a stack of unblocked-but-mechanical pieces —
codified smoke test, infra-spec retirement, documentation, and
the first real-world trial port.

## Where we are

Eight commits past the apparatus skeleton:

  b306ab98  apparatus skeleton
  d6387a24  rig template + skeleton engines
  776db61f  staged phase orchestrators
  65cc30ab  manifest CLI (`nsg lab-trial-post`)
  3cc167c1  smoke-test confirmation (live trial in vibers)
  5b4b71b8  codex fixture engines (local-bare flow)
  f93266bc  guild fixture engines
  cc83efd2  cross-guild scenario engines
  62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of all three engine pairs

Three engine pairs are real and live-verified against vibers:

  - `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}`        (c-moma9y1k)
      → engines/codex-fixture.ts
      Local bare repo per trial; no GitHub. Auto-default
      codexName = `<slug>-<writId-tail-8>`.

  - `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}`        (c-momaa03d)
      → engines/guild-fixture.ts
      `nsg init` → plugin install loop → duck-typed codex
      registration → guild.json deep-merge → file copies.
      Default guildPath = `<labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/`.

  - `lab.commission-post-xguild`
    `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` (c-momaa1vt)
      → engines/scenario-xguild.ts
      commission-post inline-polls until terminal by default
      (waitForTerminal=true). wait engine standalone for future
      multi-step scenarios.

Cross-cutting changes: phases.ts now injects `_trial:{slug,
writId, fixtureId}` into every grafted work-engine's givens —
general improvement, lights up trial-aware defaulting in all
fixture engines. Stub-engine console logging removed.

123/123 unit tests passing across the package. Two trials
posted live against vibers, both completed cleanly (11 engines
each, ~14s wallclock). The daemon currently running has all
this code loaded.

## What's left

### Blocked on the archive design conversation (c-momaa5o9)

Two engines depend on knowing where archived trial data lives.
The companion handoff at `.scratch/handoff-laboratory-archive-design.md`
walks through three options (A: lab-host books, B: sanctum
mirror, C: hybrid) and suggests a stress-test → pick → specify
flow. Until that's resolved, the following are blocked:

  - **`lab.archive`** (c-momaa5o9)
      Final consolidation step. Yields should include archive-
      target identifier (writ id if A/C, filesystem path if B)
      so the trial writ's resolution carries forward where to
      find the archive. Once landed, the teardown engines'
      archive-presence safety check tightens from "context.upstream.archive
      defined" to "archived === true".

  - **`lab.probe-stacks-dump` + `lab.probe-git-range`** (c-momaa3w7)
      Output shape depends on archive design. Stacks dump
      exports the test guild's books — JSON-per-table under (B),
      rows-into-lab-books under (A), or both under (C). Git-range
      captures commits between codex base and head SHAs (per-commit
      diffs + commits-manifest). Both probes read upstream paths
      from their context.upstream — explicit declared upstreams
      in their grafted entry.

### Unblocked

  - **Codified smoke test** (c-momaa75l)
      The live verifies in `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml`
      worked end-to-end; codify them as a recurring package
      test. Pattern: build a synthetic guild in tmp, post a
      trivial trial via the CLI, assert rig completes with
      every expected engine in the expected order, clean up.
      Look at spider's integration tests for precedent
      (`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider-test-fixture.ts`).

  - **Retire the superseded infra spec** (c-momaa8mk)
      `experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md` is
      what the apparatus replaces. Decide: historical-archive
      vs delete-since-superseded. Conclude `c-molveg89` (the
      click that tracked the spec as a P3 prereq) with a pointer
      to the apparatus.

  - **Documentation** (c-momaaa3t)
      `packages/laboratory/README.md` — flesh out manifest
      authoring, the rig flow, and the archive layout (per
      whatever the archive design picks). Apparatus doc under
      `/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/` describing the
      apparatus's place in the architecture. User-facing guide
      "evaluating prompts/plugins via lab trials" living in the
      sanctum since end-users consume sanctum-side docs.

  - **First real-world trial** (c-momaab8y)
      Likely X016 orientation suppression — see the
      cost-optimization landscape (c-mok4nke6) for the
      candidate list. Author its manifest, run it, verify
      findings land where expected. The capstone — validates
      the apparatus on real research work and produces the
      first real findings via the new pipeline. Depends on
      archive being real.

### Grit surfaced from live verify (no click yet)

Two operational concerns emerged from posting trials against
vibers. Worth opening clicks for follow-up; not blocking:

  - **Failed-trial orphan cleanup.** When `guild-setup` fails
    after `codex-setup` succeeds, the rig fails without running
    teardowns. The codex stays registered in the lab-host, the
    bare repo stays on disk. Two design directions: setup
    engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires
    on any rig terminal-state, OR teardowns run unconditionally
    on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream
    chain succeeded).

  - **Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove`
    invoked from the CLI runs in a separate process, updates
    guild.json, but the running daemon's in-memory Scriptorium
    state isn't notified. Daemon's next guild.json write
    (during the next successful trial's teardown) serializes
    its in-memory state and reverts the orphans. Vibers
    currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes
    (`verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}`) that will
    only clear on daemon restart. Cosmetic for now (orphan bare
    repos don't run code), but means CLI-side orphan cleanup
    doesn't stick.

## Implementation discipline

Same as before; preserved here for continuity:

  - **One commit per engine.** Each commit ships the engine +
    its tests + replaces the stub in `stubs.ts` + adds the real
    implementation file under `engines/`. Conclude the matching
    click in the same commit.
  - **Match framework conventions.** Each engine is an
    `EngineDesign` with `id`, `run(givens, context)`, and
    optional `retry`. Errors throw; the spider routes to engine
    failure.
  - **Givens carry the contract.** Document each engine's
    givens shape in a TS comment block at the top of the file.
    Manifest authors read those to know what to put in
    `ext.laboratory.config`.
  - **Live verification per engine.** After implementing,
    post a trial that exercises it and check the rig completes.

## Pointers to read first

  - `nsg click extract --id c-moma9llq` — full implementation
    click subtree.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/phases.ts` — phase
    orchestrators that route into each engine; now also
    documents the `InjectedTrialContext` shape that every
    fixture engine sees.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/codex-fixture.ts` — first
    real engine pair; pattern for fixture engines (validation,
    rollback, archive-presence safety check).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/guild-fixture.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out to nsg, with sequential
    plugin-install loop.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out + parse JSON +
    optionally poll.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/stubs.ts` — what's still
    a stub. The remaining `lab.archive`, `lab.probe-stacks-dump`,
    `lab.probe-git-range` are here.
  - `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` — verify manifests
    used in live testing; useful templates for the codified
    smoke test.

## Live test environment

Vibers is the lab-host guild for development. The daemon
loaded the latest code as of session cb9f1554. After plugin
code changes, restart vibers' daemon to pick up new engine
registrations. The CLI tool (`nsg lab-trial-post`) reads source
per-invocation (fresh node process), but the running spider
loaded its engines at startup.

**Caveat:** vibers currently has three orphan codexes from
the live-verify session that survived a daemon write. They
won't run anything but force `--codex` selection on
`lab-trial-post` invocations until cleared via daemon restart.

For local upstream codex testing, `/workspace/nexus-mk2` is the
natural seed (the trial codex bare repo will be a per-trial
isolated copy at a base SHA). For plugin install in the test
guild, use `file:<absolute-path>` syntax (npm-compatible);
`link:` is pnpm-only and trips EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL on the
test guilds (which default to npm).

## Companion handoff

`.scratch/handoff-laboratory-archive-design.md` — the design
conversation that unblocks the archive engine and the probes.
Three options (A: lab-host books, B: sanctum mirror, C: hybrid)
laid out with trade-offs; suggested approach is stress-test
each against a worked example, pick, then specify in the
laboratory README.

Patron decisions: codex repos go in shardworks org, --private, naming 'trial-<slug>-<trialId-prefix>'. Conclusion: Cross-guild scenario engines landed (engines/scenario-xguild.ts). lab.commission-post-xguild posts a commission via 'nsg --guild-root <test-guild> commission-post', then INLINE polls until terminal (default waitForTerminal=true) — single engine fits the rig template's single-scenario-engine slot for the canonical 'post and wait' flow. Both engines share waitForWritTerminal helper which polls 'nsg writ-show --format json' and checks classification==='terminal' to detect terminal state. Defaults: pollIntervalMs=5000, timeoutMs=1.8M (30 min), fail-loud on timeout. 22 unit tests covering pure helpers (discoverTestGuilds 5 cases, extractH1Title 6 cases) + engine validation guards (briefPath absolute, no/multi guild, malformed numbers, missing brief, writId required). > Design and implement the archive engine — open sub-question on output target (lab guild books vs sanctum mirror vs hybrid?), per-trial storage layout, linkage from trial writ to archive artifacts. Design conclusion lands in this click; implementation follows in same scope.
Status: concluded
Conclusion: DB-authoritative archive with on-demand filesystem materialization. Archive engine owns one minimal index book — lab-trial-archives — with row shape { id, trialId (FK to clerk/writs), status, archivedAt, probes[{id, engineId, summary}] }; archive engine has no schema opinions about probe data. Probes own their own books: probe-stacks-dump writes generic-bodied rows (one per source-row across test-guild books) to lab-trial-stacks-dumps; probe-git-range writes one row per captured commit (10MB per-diff hard-cap tripwire) to lab-trial-codex-commits. A new probe — lab.probe-trial-context — joins the standard probe set and captures rig id, rig template name, framework SHA, resolved plugin pins, and manifest snapshot as summary-only data (no bulk book). Trial-writ linkage is FK-only (lab-trial-archives.trialId) — no clerk link kind, since clerk links are writ-to-writ. Pure JSON1 expression indexes added per hot query (no per-row extracted columns). Atomicity via single SQLite transaction. Annotations and analysis live in sanctum markdown referencing trialId — not in any laboratory book. Probes and their books bundle in @shardworks/laboratory for MVP with code seams (per-probe directories) designed for clean v2 extraction into separate plugins. Materialization via 'nsg lab trial-extract <trialId> --to <dir>' uses a probe registry that dispatches by engineId — MVP-required, tracked at sibling click c-momkil4p. CLI surface: nsg lab trial-show, nsg lab trial-extract, nsg lab trial-export-book. Full spec landed in packages/laboratory/README.md.

## c-momaa5o9-62fbf19d185d [concluded]
> Design and implement the archive engine — open sub-question on output target (lab guild books vs sanctum mirror vs hybrid?), per-trial storage layout, linkage from trial writ to archive artifacts. Design conclusion lands in this click; implementation follows in same scope.
Status: concluded
Conclusion: DB-authoritative archive with on-demand filesystem materialization. Archive engine owns one minimal index book — lab-trial-archives — with row shape { id, trialId (FK to clerk/writs), status, archivedAt, probes[{id, engineId, summary}] }; archive engine has no schema opinions about probe data. Probes own their own books: probe-stacks-dump writes generic-bodied rows (one per source-row across test-guild books) to lab-trial-stacks-dumps; probe-git-range writes one row per captured commit (10MB per-diff hard-cap tripwire) to lab-trial-codex-commits. A new probe — lab.probe-trial-context — joins the standard probe set and captures rig id, rig template name, framework SHA, resolved plugin pins, and manifest snapshot as summary-only data (no bulk book). Trial-writ linkage is FK-only (lab-trial-archives.trialId) — no clerk link kind, since clerk links are writ-to-writ. Pure JSON1 expression indexes added per hot query (no per-row extracted columns). Atomicity via single SQLite transaction. Annotations and analysis live in sanctum markdown referencing trialId — not in any laboratory book. Probes and their books bundle in @shardworks/laboratory for MVP with code seams (per-probe directories) designed for clean v2 extraction into separate plugins. Materialization via 'nsg lab trial-extract <trialId> --to <dir>' uses a probe registry that dispatches by engineId — MVP-required, tracked at sibling click c-momkil4p. CLI surface: nsg lab trial-show, nsg lab trial-extract, nsg lab trial-export-book. Full spec landed in packages/laboratory/README.md.

file.content:
One row per archived trial. Tiny. Written-once at archive time. The
archive engine has **no schema opinions about probe data** — its job is
to record what probes ran and what each yielded as a summary.

```ts
interface LabTrialArchive {
  id: string;                            // generated
  trialId: string;                       // FK → clerk/writs (indexed)
  status: 'in-progress' | 'complete' | 'failed';
  archivedAt: string;                    // ISO

  probes: Array<{
    id: string;                          // probe id from trial config
    engineId: string;                    // e.g. 'lab.probe-stacks-dump'
    summary: Record<string, unknown>;    // opaque to archive engine
  }>;
}
```

Trial-level facts (manifest body, codex base SHA, codex upstream URL,
plugin specifications) are **not duplicated here** — they live on the
trial writ at `ext.laboratory.config`. Reproducibility-relevant runtime
facts (resolved plugin pins, framework SHA, rig template name) are
captured by `lab.probe-trial-context` and live in that probe's summary.

#### `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` — owned by `lab.probe-stacks-dump`

One row per source-row, across every book in the test guild. Generic
JSON-bodied; querying is via SQLite JSON1 expressions.

input.old_string:
### Atomicity

The archive engine wraps its work in a single SQLite transaction:
write the `lab-trial-archives` row with `status: 'in-progress'`, run
each probe (probes write to their own books inside the same
transaction via the engine context), update the row to
`status: 'complete'`. Any failure rolls back the whole transaction —
no half-state, no orphan rows.

The teardown engines (`lab.guild-teardown`, `lab.codex-teardown`)
refuse to run unless the trial's archive record exists with
`status: 'complete'`.
input.new_string:
### Atomicity

Per-engine, not per-trial. The rig grafts probes ahead of the archive
engine; each probe writes its data atomically inside its own SQLite
transaction. The archive engine runs after all probes complete and
writes the `lab-trial-archives` index row atomically once. The index
row is the join key for queries — probe-book rows without a matching
archive-index row are orphan data from trials whose rigs failed before
reaching the archive step. Orphans are safe to ignore in queries
(every analytical query starts from `lab-trial-archives` and joins
out). Cleanup of orphan probe rows is future polish; MVP leaves them
in place.

The teardown engines (`lab.guild-teardown`, `lab.codex-teardown`)
refuse to run unless the trial's archive row exists.

toolUseResult.oldString:
- session: 0cb4907e-adf3-41d2-92f3-6e88dc76565b
  date: 2026-05-01
  item: "Resolved the laboratory archive design (concludes c-momaa5o9). Decision: DB-authoritative archive with on-demand filesystem materialization — captured trial data lives in the lab guild's stacks DB, the filesystem story is provided by an extract tool that reads from the DB on demand. Pivoted from my initial hybrid (C) recommendation after Sean reframed the brittleness concern as 'patron-tree↔apparatus-data' rather than 'DB↔filesystem' abstractly — the sanctum is human-owned text under git, lab dumps are machine-owned bulk data, mixing them was the actual smell. Three books: lab-trial-archives (archive-engine-owned index, one row per trial, shape {id, trialId-FK, status, archivedAt, probes[{id, engineId, summary}]}, archive engine has no schema opinions about probe data) + lab-trial-stacks-dumps (probe-stacks-dump-owned, generic JSON-bodied row per source-row across test-guild books, queried via JSON1 expression indexes added per hot pattern) + lab-trial-codex-commits (probe-git-range-owned, one row per captured commit with diff body, 10MB per-diff hard-cap tripwire). New probe joining the standard set: lab.probe-trial-context — captures rig id, rig template name, framework SHA, resolved plugin pins, manifest snapshot as summary-only data; folds into c-momaa3w7 implementation scope. Trial-writ linkage is FK-only (caught a real flaw — clerk's WritLinkDoc is writ-to-writ only via sourceId/targetId; archive records aren't writs, so my earlier 'laboratory.archived-as' link kind couldn't have worked). CLI surface: nsg lab trial-show / trial-extract / trial-export-book (renamed from export-jsonl). Probe-extraction dispatch via a probe registry — Sean rejected MVP-hardcoding ('the type of thing we will forget the intent by the time we need to add the lookup'), so the registry is MVP-required, opened as new sibling click c-momkil4p under c-moma9llq. Bundled-with-clean-seams packaging for MVP: probes and their books all live in @shardworks/laboratory but organized per-probe (src/probes/<name>/{engine.ts, book.ts, extractor.ts}) so future per-plugin extraction is a mechanical lift. Atomicity via single SQLite transaction; teardown gate on archive status==='complete'. Annotations live sanctum-side referencing trialId, not in any laboratory book. Full spec written to packages/laboratory/README.md as the canonical home. Conversational arc: pressure-tested A/B/C with worked example of a P3 trial, recommended C, took Sean's reframe to A, then took three more Sean pokes (scenarioWritId being scenario-specific, codex URL belongs on writ not duplicated, metadata-as-flat-bag mixing probe-yields with apparatus-facts, books being too specific) that revealed the deeper architectural fix — archive engine is a minimal index, probes own their own books and extraction. Companion handoff (.scratch/handoff-laboratory-implementation.md) retained as the next thread's anchor; archive-design handoff scratch deleted now that the spec is in its permanent home."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

- session: 0cb4907e-adf3-41d2-92f3-6e88dc76565b
  date: 2026-05-01
  item: "Tightened archive design before implementation handoff — caught a real architectural inconsistency in my README v1: the 'single SQLite transaction wrapping archive engine + all probes' atomicity story doesn't work given the rig template grafts probes BEFORE archive as separate engines (each engine is its own transaction by construction). Fixed: per-engine atomicity, archive row writes once on success, orphan probe rows from rig-failed trials are tolerated (every analytical query joins from lab-trial-archives so orphans don't surface). Dropped the 'status: in-progress | complete | failed' field from the LabTrialArchive schema — vestigial once we accept atomic-per-row writes; row existence IS the success signal. Teardown gate language updated: 'archive row exists for this trialId' (was 'status===complete'). Click hygiene: amended c-momaa3w7 to cover the three probes with the new storage shape (was stale 'JSON-per-table' wording from the pre-design conversation handoff); opened c-momkqtn5 as the implementation click for the archive engine itself (the original c-momaa5o9 conflated design+implementation; concluding it on design alone left implementation untracked)."
  commissionable: false
  justification: sanctum

input.command:
git add packages/laboratory/README.md experiments/data/coco-log.yaml && GIT_AUTHOR_NAME=Coco GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL=coco@nexus.local git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
laboratory: tighten archive atomicity model

Caught an architectural inconsistency before the implementation
session picks this up: the 'single SQLite transaction wrapping archive
+ probes' story I wrote in v1 doesn't survive the rig structure (rig
template grafts probes ahead of archive as separate engines, so each
engine is its own transaction).

Fixed:
- Per-engine atomicity. Each probe writes its data atomically inside
  its own transaction; archive engine writes the index row atomically
  once, after all probes complete.
- Orphan tolerance. Probe-book rows from rig-failed trials are tolerated;
  every analytical query joins from lab-trial-archives, so orphans
  don't surface. Cleanup is future polish.
- Dropped vestigial `status` field from LabTrialArchive — row existence
  IS the success signal.
- Teardown gate now reads as 'archive row exists for this trialId'.

Click hygiene also: c-momaa3w7 amended to cover the three probes with
the new storage shape; c-momkqtn5 opened for the archive engine
implementation (the original design click conflated design+impl;
concluding on design alone left impl untracked).

Session: 0cb4907e-adf3-41d2-92f3-6e88dc76565b
EOF
)"

content.text:
Done. Implementation is now genuinely unblocked. Here's the handoff summary:

---

# Laboratory archive design — implementer handoff

**Decision:** DB-authoritative archive with on-demand filesystem materialization. **Full spec:** `packages/laboratory/README.md` → "Archive design" section.

## Architectural shape in one paragraph

Trial data lives in the lab guild's stacks DB. Three books: `lab-trial-archives` (one row per archived trial — the index), `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` (generic JSON-bodied row per source-row across test-guild books), `lab-trial-codex-commits` (one row per commit, diff body, 10MB cap). Archive engine owns only the index book and has no schema opinions about probe data; probes own their own books. Trial-writ linkage is FK only (`lab-trial-archives.trialId` → `clerk/writs.id`). Filesystem materialization happens on demand via `nsg lab trial-extract`, which dispatches per-probe extractors via a probe registry.

## What's left to implement (under `c-moma9llq`)

| Click | Scope |
|---|---|
| `c-momkil4p` | **Probe registry + extract dispatch** (MVP-required). Probe engines self-declare an `extract(trialId, targetDir, guild)` handler; `nsg lab trial-extract` looks them up by `engineId`. |
| `c-momaa3w7` | **Three standard probes**: `lab.probe-stacks-dump` (test-guild books → generic rows), `lab.probe-git-range` (codex commits → diff rows), `lab.probe-trial-context` (rig+template+framework+pins+manifest snapshot, summary-only). Each probe under `src/probes/<name>/{engine,book,extractor}.ts`. |
| `c-momkqtn5` | **Archive engine** (`lab.archive`). Reads probe yields from `context.upstream`, writes one row to `lab-trial-archives`. Atomic per-row. Updates teardown gate from "context.upstream.archive defined" to "archive row exists." |
| `c-momaa75l` | **Codified smoke test.** End-to-end pipeline test. |
| `c-momaaa3t` | **Documentation** (architecture doc, end-user guide). |
| `c-momaab8y` | **First real-world trial port** — probably X016. |

## Build order suggestion

1. **Probe registry shape** (small, MVP-required, gates extract tool).
2. **Probes in parallel** — each registers with the registry on plugin start. Stacks-dump and git-range have stub equivalents to replace; trial-context is greenfield.
3. **Archive engine** — consumes probe upstream yields; replaces the stub.
4. **Extract / show / export-book CLI tools** — implementable any time the registry exists; can develop alongside probes.
5. **Smoke test** — codify after the chain works end-to-end.

## Key contracts (full details in README)

- **Atomicity:** per-engine. No cross-engine transactions. Archive row is atomic; probe rows are atomic per-probe. Orphans tolerated (all queries join from `lab-trial-archives`).
- **Indexing:** pure JSON1 expression indexes. Add per hot query, don't extract columns at write time.
- **Big-diff tripwire:** `lab.probe-git-range` fails loud on any single diff > 10MB.
- **Linkage:** FK only — clerk links are writ-to-writ and archive records aren't writs.
- **Annotations:** live in sanctum markdown referencing `trialId`, never in laboratory books.
- **Packaging:** all built-in probes bundle in `@shardworks/laboratory` for MVP; per-probe directory layout keeps the v2 plugin-extraction lift mechanical.

## Companion docs

- `.scratch/handoff-laboratory-implementation.md` — the full implementation handoff with engine patterns and live-test environment notes.
- `c-momaa5o9` (concluded) — design-decision context if you want the reasoning trail.

input.content:
# Handoff — Laboratory implementation, continuing

The implementation thread that started with the apparatus
skeleton has now landed three real engine pairs and a resolved
archive design. What's left: the archive engine, the three
standard probes, the probe registry + extract tool, and a
stack of mechanical pieces (codified smoke test, infra-spec
retirement, documentation, first real-world trial port).

## Where we are

Nine commits past the apparatus skeleton:

  b306ab98  apparatus skeleton
  d6387a24  rig template + skeleton engines
  776db61f  staged phase orchestrators
  65cc30ab  manifest CLI (`nsg lab-trial-post`)
  3cc167c1  smoke-test confirmation (live trial in vibers)
  5b4b71b8  codex fixture engines (local-bare flow)
  f93266bc  guild fixture engines
  cc83efd2  cross-guild scenario engines
  62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of all three engine pairs
  082ad189  archive design — DB-authoritative + extract tool
  34b328ae  archive design — atomicity tightening

Three engine pairs are real and live-verified against vibers:

  - `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}`        (c-moma9y1k)
      → engines/codex-fixture.ts
      Local bare repo per trial; no GitHub. Auto-default
      codexName = `<slug>-<writId-tail-8>`.

  - `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}`        (c-momaa03d)
      → engines/guild-fixture.ts
      `nsg init` → plugin install loop → duck-typed codex
      registration → guild.json deep-merge → file copies.
      Default guildPath = `<labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/`.

  - `lab.commission-post-xguild`
    `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` (c-momaa1vt)
      → engines/scenario-xguild.ts
      commission-post inline-polls until terminal by default
      (waitForTerminal=true). wait engine standalone for future
      multi-step scenarios.

Cross-cutting changes: phases.ts now injects `_trial:{slug,
writId, fixtureId}` into every grafted work-engine's givens —
general improvement, lights up trial-aware defaulting in all
fixture engines. Stub-engine console logging removed.

123/123 unit tests passing across the package. Two trials
posted live against vibers, both completed cleanly (11 engines
each, ~14s wallclock). The daemon currently running has all
this code loaded.

The archive design conversation also wrapped (c-momaa5o9
concluded). Full spec landed in `packages/laboratory/README.md`
under "Archive design." High-level shape:

  - **DB-authoritative.** Captured trial data lives in the lab
    guild's stacks DB, not the sanctum filesystem.
  - **Three books.** `lab-trial-archives` (archive-engine-owned
    index, one row per trial); `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` (probe-
    stacks-dump-owned, generic JSON-bodied rows); `lab-trial-
    codex-commits` (probe-git-range-owned, one row per commit
    with 10MB diff cap).
  - **Probes own their own books.** Archive engine has no
    schema opinions about probe data — it just records which
    probes ran and what each yielded as a summary.
  - **FK linkage only.** `lab-trial-archives.trialId` → 
    `clerk/writs.id`. No clerk link kind (clerk links are
    writ-to-writ; archive records aren't writs).
  - **On-demand filesystem materialization.** `nsg lab trial-
    extract <trialId> --to <dir>` reads from books and writes
    files; probe registry dispatches per-probe `extract()`
    handlers.

## What's left

All previously-blocked work is now unblocked. Click-by-click:

### Probe registry + extract dispatch (`c-momkil4p`) — MVP-required

The seam that lets `nsg lab trial-extract` be probe-pluggable
without hardcoded knowledge. Probe engines self-declare an
`extract(trialId, targetDir, guild) → { files: [...] }`
handler; the laboratory plugin builds a probe registry from
per-probe registrations on plugin start; the extract tool
dispatches by `engineId` via the registry.

Suggested as the first piece because it gates the extract tool
and shapes the probe-engine interface.

### Three standard probe engines (`c-momaa3w7`)

  - **`lab.probe-stacks-dump`** — walks every book in the test
    guild, writes one row per source-row to `lab-trial-stacks-
    dumps`. Summary: `{ bookCounts: { '<plugin>/<book>': N } }`.
    Test guild discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream`
    (any yield with `{guildName, guildPath}`), same pattern as
    scenario-xguild.

  - **`lab.probe-git-range`** — captures commits between codex
    base and head SHAs, writes one row per commit to `lab-trial-
    codex-commits`. Summary: `{ headSha, commitCount,
    totalDiffBytes }`. **Fails loud on any single diff > 10MB**
    (tripwire, not a constraint we expect to hit). Codex
    discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream` (any yield
    with `{codexName, remoteUrl}`).

  - **`lab.probe-trial-context`** — captures rig id, rig
    template name, framework SHA, resolved plugin pins,
    manifest snapshot. Summary-as-data; no bulk book. Reads
    rig metadata from `context.rigId`, manifest from the trial
    writ's `ext.laboratory.config`. Included in the default
    rig template.

Each probe's code lives under `src/probes/<name>/{engine.ts,
book.ts, extractor.ts}` — per-probe directory keeps the v2
"lift into separate plugin" path mechanical.

### Archive engine (`c-momkqtn5`)

`lab.archive` — reads probe summaries from `context.upstream`
and writes one atomic row to `lab-trial-archives` with
`{id, trialId, archivedAt, probes: [{id, engineId, summary}]}`.
**Per-engine atomicity** (the rig grafts probes before archive
as separate engines, so each engine is its own transaction;
archive row writes once on success). Trials whose rigs fail
before reaching archive simply have no matching row;
analytical queries join from `lab-trial-archives` outward, so
orphan probe rows don't surface.

The teardown engines' archive-presence safety check tightens:
**from** "context.upstream.archive defined" **to** "archive row
exists for this trialId."

### CLI tools

Three new tools, all with the `lab-` prefix so they auto-group
under `nsg lab <subcommand>` once installed (the framework's
auto-grouping activates when ≥2 tools share a prefix; we
currently have one — `lab-trial-post` — and its CLI form is
`nsg lab-trial-post`):

  - `lab-trial-show <trialId>` — print archive metadata + probe
    summaries from `lab-trial-archives`.
  - `lab-trial-extract <trialId> --to <path> [--force]` —
    materialize captured data to a directory; refuses to
    overwrite without `--force`. Probe registry dispatches
    per-probe extractors.
  - `lab-trial-export-book <trialId> --book <name> [--format
    jsonl|json]` — stream one source book for analysis pipelines.
    Default `jsonl`.

### Codified smoke test (`c-momaa75l`)

The live verifies in `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` worked
end-to-end; codify them as a recurring package test. Pattern:
build a synthetic guild in tmp, post a trivial trial via the
CLI, assert rig completes with every expected engine in the
expected order, then assert the archive row + probe data
exist, clean up. Look at spider's integration tests for
precedent (`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider-
test-fixture.ts`).

### Retire the superseded infra spec (`c-momaa8mk`)

`experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md` is what the
apparatus replaces. Decide: historical-archive vs delete-since-
superseded. Conclude `c-molveg89` (the click that tracked the
spec as a P3 prereq) with a pointer to the apparatus.

### Documentation (`c-momaaa3t`)

  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — already carries the
    architecture overview and archive design. Flesh out with
    manifest authoring, the rig flow narrative, and end-user
    examples.
  - Apparatus doc under `/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/`
    describing the apparatus's place in the architecture.
  - User-facing guide "evaluating prompts/plugins via lab
    trials" living in the sanctum since end-users consume
    sanctum-side docs.

### First real-world trial (`c-momaab8y`)

Likely X016 orientation suppression — see the cost-optimization
landscape (`c-mok4nke6`) for the candidate list. Author its
manifest, run it, verify findings land in
`lab-trial-stacks-dumps` and `lab-trial-codex-commits`, extract
to a directory, run analysis. The capstone — validates the
apparatus on real research work and produces the first real
findings via the new pipeline.

## Suggested build order

1. **Probe registry shape** (small, MVP-required, gates extract
   tool).
2. **Three probes in parallel.** Stacks-dump and git-range
   replace stubs; trial-context is greenfield. Each registers
   with the registry on plugin start.
3. **Archive engine.** Consumes probe upstream yields; replaces
   the stub.
4. **Extract / show / export-book CLI tools.** Buildable any
   time the registry exists; can develop alongside probes.
5. **Codified smoke test.** After the chain works end-to-end.
6. **Documentation + first real-world trial port.** Capstone.

## Grit surfaced from live verify (no click yet)

Two operational concerns emerged from posting trials against
vibers. Worth opening clicks for follow-up; not blocking:

  - **Failed-trial orphan cleanup.** When `guild-setup` fails
    after `codex-setup` succeeds, the rig fails without running
    teardowns. The codex stays registered in the lab-host, the
    bare repo stays on disk. Two design directions: setup
    engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires
    on any rig terminal-state, OR teardowns run unconditionally
    on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream
    chain succeeded).

  - **Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove`
    invoked from the CLI runs in a separate process, updates
    guild.json, but the running daemon's in-memory Scriptorium
    state isn't notified. Daemon's next guild.json write
    (during the next successful trial's teardown) serializes
    its in-memory state and reverts the orphans. Vibers
    currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes
    (`verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}`) that will
    only clear on daemon restart. Cosmetic for now (orphan bare
    repos don't run code), but means CLI-side orphan cleanup
    doesn't stick.

## Implementation discipline

Same as before; preserved here for continuity:

  - **One commit per engine.** Each commit ships the engine +
    its tests + replaces the stub in `stubs.ts` + adds the real
    implementation file under `engines/` (or `src/probes/<name>/`
    for probes). Conclude the matching click in the same commit.
  - **Match framework conventions.** Each engine is an
    `EngineDesign` with `id`, `run(givens, context)`, and
    optional `retry`. Probes additionally provide `extract()`
    per the registry contract.
  - **Givens carry the contract.** Document each engine's
    givens shape in a TS comment block at the top of the file.
    Manifest authors read those to know what to put in
    `ext.laboratory.config`.
  - **Live verification per engine.** After implementing, post
    a trial that exercises it and check the rig completes.
  - **Atomicity is per-engine, not per-trial.** Don't try to
    span SQLite transactions across engines (the rig structure
    forbids it). Probes write atomically per-probe; archive
    writes atomically per-row; orphan probe rows are tolerated.

## Pointers to read first

  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — **canonical archive design
    spec.** Architecture, book schemas, atomicity, registry
    contract, CLI surface. The single source of truth.
  - `nsg click extract --id c-moma9llq` — full implementation
    click subtree.
  - `nsg click show c-momaa5o9` — design conclusion (the
    reasoning trail behind the README spec).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/phases.ts` — phase
    orchestrators that route into each engine; documents the
    `InjectedTrialContext` shape every fixture engine sees.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/codex-fixture.ts` — first
    real engine pair; pattern for fixture engines (validation,
    rollback, archive-presence safety check).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/guild-fixture.ts` — pattern
    for engines that shell out to nsg, with sequential plugin-
    install loop.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out + parse JSON +
    optionally poll.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/stubs.ts` — what's still a
    stub. The remaining `lab.archive`, `lab.probe-stacks-dump`,
    `lab.probe-git-range` are here. `lab.probe-trial-context`
    is greenfield (no stub yet — add it during probe work).
  - `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` — verify manifests used
    in live testing; useful templates for the codified smoke
    test.

## Live test environment

Vibers is the lab-host guild for development. The daemon loaded
the latest code as of session cb9f1554 (the three engine pair
implementations). After plugin code changes, restart vibers'
daemon to pick up new engine registrations. The CLI tool
(`nsg lab-trial-post`) reads source per-invocation (fresh node
process), but the running spider loaded its engines at startup.

**Caveat:** vibers currently has three orphan codexes from the
live-verify session that survived a daemon write. They won't
run anything but force `--codex` selection on `lab-trial-post`
invocations until cleared via daemon restart.

For local upstream codex testing, `/workspace/nexus-mk2` is the
natural seed (the trial codex bare repo will be a per-trial
isolated copy at a base SHA). For plugin install in the test
guild, use `file:<absolute-path>` syntax (npm-compatible);
`link:` is pnpm-only and trips EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL on the test
guilds (which default to npm).

**CLI surface evolution.** Currently only `lab-trial-post`
exists, so the CLI displays it as `nsg lab-trial-post` (single-
tool form). Once the new tools (`lab-trial-show`,
`lab-trial-extract`, `lab-trial-export-book`) land, the
framework's auto-grouping activates (≥2 tools sharing a prefix)
and the CLI surfaces them all under `nsg lab <subcommand>` —
including the existing one becoming `nsg lab trial-post`. This
is a presentation change driven by the framework, not anything
the laboratory plugin controls.

# Handoff — Laboratory implementation, continuing

The implementation thread that started with the apparatus
skeleton has now landed three real engine pairs. What's left is
the archive engine, the probes, the codified smoke test, infra-
spec retirement, documentation, and the first real-world trial
port. The archive-design conversation has been resolved (see
click `c-momaa5o9` and `packages/laboratory/README.md`); this
handoff is purely incremental implementation against that
specification.

## Where we are

Eight commits past the apparatus skeleton:

  b306ab98  apparatus skeleton
  d6387a24  rig template + skeleton engines
  776db61f  staged phase orchestrators
  65cc30ab  manifest CLI (`nsg lab-trial-post`)
  3cc167c1  smoke-test confirmation (live trial in vibers)
  5b4b71b8  codex fixture engines (local-bare flow)
  f93266bc  guild fixture engines
  cc83efd2  cross-guild scenario engines
  62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of all three engine pairs

Three engine pairs are real and live-verified against vibers:

  - `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}`        (c-moma9y1k)
      → engines/codex-fixture.ts
      Local bare repo per trial; no GitHub. Auto-default
      codexName = `<slug>-<writId-tail-8>`.

  - `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}`        (c-momaa03d)
      → engines/guild-fixture.ts
      `nsg init` → plugin install loop → duck-typed codex
      registration → guild.json deep-merge → file copies.
      Default guildPath = `<labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/`.

  - `lab.commission-post-xguild`
    `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` (c-momaa1vt)
      → engines/scenario-xguild.ts
      commission-post inline-polls until terminal by default
      (waitForTerminal=true). wait engine standalone for future
      multi-step scenarios.

Cross-cutting changes: phases.ts injects `_trial:{slug, writId,
fixtureId}` into every grafted work-engine's givens — general
improvement, lights up trial-aware defaulting in all fixture
engines. Stub-engine console logging removed.

123/123 unit tests passing. Two trials posted live against
vibers, both completed cleanly (11 engines each, ~14s
wallclock).

## Suggested order

Archive first, then probes against it, then the cleanup tail.
Each engine pair lives behind a click; one commit per engine
pair as before.

### 1. `lab.archive` (`c-momaa5o9` impl)

Final consolidation step. The archive design conversation has
resolved the storage layout (see the click conclusion and
`packages/laboratory/README.md` for the chosen shape — books
vs sanctum mirror vs hybrid — and the row/path schemas).
Implement the engine to produce that shape; yields should
include the archive-target identifier (writ id, filesystem
path, or both per the design) so the trial writ's resolution
carries forward where to find the archive.

After this engine completes, the codex-teardown and guild-
teardown engines' archive-presence safety checks tighten from
"context.upstream.archive defined" to "archived === true".
The check is documented inline in both engines pointing at
this click.

The engine reads probe outputs from `context.upstream` —
duck-typed discovery in the same vein as guild-fixture's codex
discovery and scenario-xguild's test-guild discovery: any
upstream entry whose yields match the probe-output shape gets
consolidated.

### 2. `lab.probe-stacks-dump` + `lab.probe-git-range` (`c-momaa3w7`)

Implement after the archive engine — the probes' yield shape
follows the archive's input contract (rows-into-books, paths-
on-filesystem, or both per the chosen storage layout).

  - **`lab.probe-stacks-dump`** — exports the test guild's
    books. Reads the test guild path from `context.upstream`
    (any `{guildName, guildPath}` yield). Walks every book the
    test guild has and emits its rows in the agreed-upon shape.

  - **`lab.probe-git-range`** — captures commits between the
    codex base SHA and the codex head SHA. Reads the codex info
    from `context.upstream` (any `{codexName, baseSha, headSha,
    bareLocalPath}` yield). Produces per-commit diffs +
    commits-manifest. Generic enough to be useful beyond this
    apparatus — consider promoting later.

Both probes use the duck-typed upstream-discovery pattern that
guild-fixture and scenario-xguild already established; no
manifest-level dependsOn declaration of "this is the codex /
test-guild" needed.

### 3. Codified smoke test (`c-momaa75l`)

The live verifies in `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` worked
end-to-end and are useful as templates. Codify them as a
recurring package test: build a synthetic guild in tmp, post a
trivial trial via the CLI, assert the rig completes with every
expected engine in the expected order, clean up. Look at
spider's integration tests for precedent
(`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider-test-fixture.ts`).

The smoke test should exercise the full pipeline (fixtures →
scenario → probes → archive → teardowns), not just the bits
that previous unit tests covered.

### 4. Retire the superseded infra spec (`c-momaa8mk`)

`experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md` is what the
apparatus replaces. Decide: historical-archive vs delete-since-
superseded. Conclude `c-molveg89` (the click that tracked the
spec as a P3 prerequisite) with a pointer to the apparatus.

### 5. Documentation (`c-momaaa3t`)

  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — already touched in the
    archive design; flesh out manifest authoring, the rig flow,
    and the archive layout if not fully captured already.
  - Apparatus doc under `/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/`
    describing the apparatus's place in the architecture.
  - User-facing guide "evaluating prompts/plugins via lab
    trials" — sanctum-side, since end-users consume sanctum
    docs.

### 6. Port the first real-world trial (`c-momaab8y`)

Pick the next P3 experiment — likely X016 orientation
suppression (see cost-optimization landscape at click
`c-mok4nke6` for the candidate list). Author its manifest,
run it, verify findings land where expected.

This is the capstone — validates the apparatus on real
research work and produces the first real findings via the
new pipeline. Likely surfaces missed assumptions in the
archive layout or probe outputs that a synthetic smoke test
won't catch; expect a small iteration loop on the first run.

## Grit surfaced from live verify (no click yet)

Two operational concerns emerged from posting trials against
vibers. Worth opening clicks for follow-up; not blocking
the steps above:

  - **Failed-trial orphan cleanup.** When `guild-setup` fails
    after `codex-setup` succeeds, the rig fails without running
    teardowns. The codex stays registered in the lab-host, the
    bare repo stays on disk. Two design directions: setup
    engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires
    on any rig terminal-state, OR teardowns run unconditionally
    on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream
    chain succeeded).

  - **Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove`
    from the CLI runs in a separate process, updates guild.json,
    but the running daemon's in-memory Scriptorium state isn't
    notified. Daemon's next guild.json write reverts the
    orphans. Vibers currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes
    (`verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}`) that will
    only clear on daemon restart. Cosmetic (orphan bare repos
    don't run code), but means CLI-side cleanup doesn't stick.

## Implementation discipline

  - **One commit per engine.** Each commit ships the engine +
    its tests + replaces the stub in `stubs.ts` + adds the real
    implementation file under `engines/`. Conclude the matching
    click in the same commit.
  - **Match framework conventions.** Each engine is an
    `EngineDesign` with `id`, `run(givens, context)`, and
    optional `retry`. Errors throw; the spider routes to engine
    failure.
  - **Givens carry the contract.** Document each engine's
    givens shape in a TS comment block at the top of the file.
    Manifest authors read those to know what to put in
    `ext.laboratory.config`.
  - **Use injected `_trial` context for trial-aware defaulting.**
    phases.ts populates it; engines read it for sensible
    name/path defaults. Codex and guild fixtures already
    establish the pattern.
  - **Use upstream duck-typing for cross-engine wiring.** No
    manifest-level "this is the codex / test guild" declarations
    — engines walk `context.upstream` looking for yields that
    match the shape they need. Codex discovery, test-guild
    discovery, and probe discovery all follow this pattern.
  - **Live verification per engine.** After implementing, post
    a trial that exercises it and check the rig completes. The
    smoke-test pattern (write tmp manifest, `nsg lab-trial-post`,
    poll `nsg writ show --format json` until classification ===
    'terminal') works for one-off verification before the
    codified test lands.

## Pointers to read first

  - `nsg click extract --id c-moma9llq` — full implementation
    click subtree.
  - `nsg click show c-momaa5o9` — archive design conclusion.
  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — archive layout
    specification.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/phases.ts` — phase
    orchestrators that route into each engine; now also
    documents the `InjectedTrialContext` shape that every
    grafted work engine sees.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/codex-fixture.ts` — first
    real engine pair; pattern for fixture engines (validation,
    rollback, archive-presence safety check, `_trial`-driven
    defaulting).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/guild-fixture.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out to nsg, with sequential
    plugin-install loop and duck-typed codex discovery from
    upstream.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out, parse JSON, optionally
    poll until classification-terminal.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/stubs.ts` — what's still
    a stub. `lab.archive`, `lab.probe-stacks-dump`, and
    `lab.probe-git-range` are here.
  - `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` — verify manifests
    used in live testing; useful templates for the codified
    smoke test.

## Live test environment

Vibers is the lab-host guild for development. After plugin code
changes, restart vibers' daemon to pick up new engine
registrations. The CLI tool (`nsg lab-trial-post`) reads source
per-invocation (fresh node process), but the running spider
loaded its engines at startup.

**Caveat:** vibers currently has three orphan codexes from
the previous live-verify session that survived a daemon write.
They won't run anything but force `--codex` selection on
`lab-trial-post` invocations until cleared via daemon restart.

For local upstream codex testing, `/workspace/nexus-mk2` is the
natural seed (the trial codex bare repo will be a per-trial
isolated copy at a base SHA). For plugin install in the test
guild, use `file:<absolute-path>` syntax (npm-compatible);
`link:` is pnpm-only and trips EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL on the
test guilds (which default to npm).

the first real-world trial port. The archive-design conversation
has been resolved (see click `c-momaa5o9` and
`packages/laboratory/README.md`); this handoff is purely
incremental implementation against that specification.

## Where we are

Ten commits past the apparatus skeleton:

  b306ab98  apparatus skeleton
  d6387a24  rig template + skeleton engines
  776db61f  staged phase orchestrators
  65cc30ab  manifest CLI (`nsg lab-trial-post`)
  3cc167c1  smoke-test confirmation (live trial in vibers)
  5b4b71b8  codex fixture engines (local-bare flow)
  f93266bc  guild fixture engines
  cc83efd2  cross-guild scenario engines
  62abff2d  coco-log: live verify of all three engine pairs
  082ad189  archive design — DB-authoritative + extract tool
  34b328ae  archive design — atomicity tightening

Three engine pairs are real and live-verified against vibers:

  - `lab.codex-{setup,teardown}`        (c-moma9y1k)
      → engines/codex-fixture.ts
      Local bare repo per trial; no GitHub. Auto-default
      codexName = `<slug>-<writId-tail-8>`.

  - `lab.guild-{setup,teardown}`        (c-momaa03d)
      → engines/guild-fixture.ts
      `nsg init` → plugin install loop → duck-typed codex
      registration → guild.json deep-merge → file copies.
      Default guildPath = `<labHost>/.nexus/laboratory/guilds/<name>/`.

  - `lab.commission-post-xguild`
    `lab.wait-for-writ-terminal-xguild` (c-momaa1vt)
      → engines/scenario-xguild.ts
      commission-post inline-polls until terminal by default
      (waitForTerminal=true). wait engine standalone for future
      multi-step scenarios.

Cross-cutting changes: phases.ts injects `_trial:{slug, writId,
fixtureId}` into every grafted work-engine's givens — general
improvement, lights up trial-aware defaulting in all fixture
engines. Stub-engine console logging removed.

123/123 unit tests passing. Two trials posted live against
vibers, both completed cleanly (11 engines each, ~14s
wallclock).

The archive design conversation also wrapped (`c-momaa5o9`
concluded). High-level shape, with the full spec at
`packages/laboratory/README.md` → "Archive design":

  - **DB-authoritative.** Captured trial data lives in the lab
    guild's stacks DB, not the sanctum filesystem.
  - **Three books.** `lab-trial-archives` (archive-engine-owned
    index, one row per trial); `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` (probe-
    stacks-dump-owned, generic JSON-bodied rows from test-guild
    books); `lab-trial-codex-commits` (probe-git-range-owned,
    one row per commit with 10MB diff cap).
  - **Probes own their own books.** Archive engine has no
    schema opinions about probe data — it just records which
    probes ran and what each yielded as a summary.
  - **FK linkage only.** `lab-trial-archives.trialId` →
    `clerk/writs.id`. No clerk link kind (clerk links are
    writ-to-writ; archive records aren't writs).
  - **On-demand filesystem materialization.** `nsg lab trial-
    extract <trialId> --to <dir>` reads from books and writes
    files; probe registry dispatches per-probe `extract()`
    handlers.

## Suggested order

Registry first (gates the probe interface and the extract
tool), then the three probes in parallel (each registers with
the registry), then the archive engine (consumes probe
upstream yields), then the CLI tools, then cleanup tail. One
commit per engine pair as before.

### 1. Probe registry + extract dispatch (`c-momkil4p`) — MVP-required

The seam that lets `nsg lab trial-extract` be probe-pluggable
without hardcoded knowledge. Probe engines self-declare an
`extract(trialId, targetDir, guild) → { files: [...] }`
handler; the laboratory plugin builds a probe registry from
per-probe registrations on plugin start; the extract tool
dispatches by `engineId` via the registry.

This is the first piece because it shapes the probe-engine
interface (every probe lands on top of it) and gates the
extract tool. Small but load-bearing.

### 2. Three standard probes (`c-momaa3w7`)

Each probe lives under `src/probes/<name>/{engine.ts, book.ts,
extractor.ts}` — per-probe directory keeps the v2 "lift into
separate plugin" path mechanical. Each registers with the
probe registry on plugin start.

  - **`lab.probe-stacks-dump`** — walks every book in the test
    guild, writes one row per source-row to `lab-trial-stacks-
    dumps`. Summary: `{ bookCounts: { '<plugin>/<book>': N } }`.
    Test guild discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream`
    (any yield with `{guildName, guildPath}`), same pattern as
    scenario-xguild.

  - **`lab.probe-git-range`** — captures commits between codex
    base and head SHAs, writes one row per commit to `lab-trial-
    codex-commits`. Summary: `{ headSha, commitCount,
    totalDiffBytes }`. **Fails loud on any single diff > 10MB**
    (tripwire, not a constraint we expect to hit). Codex
    discovery via duck-typing on `context.upstream` (any yield
    with `{codexName, ...}`); read SHAs and the bare-repo path
    from whatever the codex-fixture yields.

  - **`lab.probe-trial-context`** — captures rig id, rig
    template name, framework SHA, resolved plugin pins,
    manifest snapshot. Summary-as-data; no bulk book. Reads
    rig metadata from `context.rigId`, manifest from the trial
    writ's `ext.laboratory.config`. Greenfield (no stub yet —
    add it during this work). Should be included in the
    default rig template.

Probes can develop in parallel — they're independent of each
other. Stacks-dump and git-range replace existing stubs in
`stubs.ts`; trial-context is brand new.

### 3. Archive engine (`c-momkqtn5`)

`lab.archive` — reads probe summaries from `context.upstream`
and writes one atomic row to `lab-trial-archives` with
`{id, trialId, archivedAt, probes: [{id, engineId, summary}]}`.
**Per-engine atomicity** (the rig grafts probes before archive
as separate engines, so each engine is its own transaction;
archive row writes once on success). Trials whose rigs fail
before reaching archive simply have no matching row;
analytical queries join from `lab-trial-archives` outward, so
orphan probe rows don't surface.

The teardown engines' archive-presence safety check tightens:
**from** "context.upstream.archive defined" **to** "archive row
exists for this trialId" — query `lab-trial-archives WHERE
trialId=?` and require non-empty.

### 4. CLI tools (folds into `c-momkil4p` for `trial-extract`; new tools for show/export-book)

Three new tools, all with the `lab-` prefix:

  - `lab-trial-show <trialId>` — print archive metadata + probe
    summaries from `lab-trial-archives`.
  - `lab-trial-extract <trialId> --to <path> [--force]` —
    materialize captured data to a directory; refuses to
    overwrite without `--force`. Probe registry dispatches
    per-probe extractors.
  - `lab-trial-export-book <trialId> --book <name> [--format
    jsonl|json]` — stream one source book for analysis pipelines.
    Default `jsonl`.

Once a second `lab-` tool ships, the framework's auto-grouping
activates (≥2 tools sharing a prefix) and the CLI surfaces
them all under `nsg lab <subcommand>` — including the existing
`lab-trial-post` becoming `nsg lab trial-post`. This is a
presentation change driven by the framework, not anything the
laboratory plugin controls.

### 5. Codified smoke test (`c-momaa75l`)

The live verifies in `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` worked
end-to-end and are useful as templates. Codify them as a
recurring package test: build a synthetic guild in tmp, post a
trivial trial via the CLI, assert the rig completes with every
expected engine in the expected order, **assert the archive
row + probe data exist**, clean up. Look at spider's
integration tests for precedent
(`/workspace/nexus/packages/plugins/spider/src/spider-test-fixture.ts`).

The smoke test should exercise the full pipeline (fixtures →
scenario → probes → archive → teardowns), not just the bits
that previous unit tests covered.

### 6. Retire the superseded infra spec (`c-momaa8mk`)

`experiments/infrastructure/setup-and-artifacts.md` is what the
apparatus replaces. Decide: historical-archive vs delete-since-
superseded. Conclude `c-molveg89` (the click that tracked the
spec as a P3 prerequisite) with a pointer to the apparatus.

### 7. Documentation (`c-momaaa3t`)

  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — already carries the
    architecture overview and archive design. Flesh out manifest
    authoring, the rig flow narrative, and end-user examples.
  - Apparatus doc under `/workspace/nexus/docs/architecture/`
    describing the apparatus's place in the architecture.
  - User-facing guide "evaluating prompts/plugins via lab
    trials" — sanctum-side, since end-users consume sanctum
    docs.

### 8. Port the first real-world trial (`c-momaab8y`)

Pick the next P3 experiment — likely X016 orientation
suppression (see cost-optimization landscape at click
`c-mok4nke6` for the candidate list). Author its manifest, run
it, verify findings land in `lab-trial-stacks-dumps` and
`lab-trial-codex-commits`, extract to a directory, run analysis.

This is the capstone — validates the apparatus on real research
work and produces the first real findings via the new pipeline.
Likely surfaces missed assumptions in the archive layout or
probe outputs that a synthetic smoke test won't catch; expect
a small iteration loop on the first run.

## Grit surfaced from live verify (no click yet)

vibers. Worth opening clicks for follow-up; not blocking the
steps above:

  - **Failed-trial orphan cleanup.** When `guild-setup` fails
    after `codex-setup` succeeds, the rig fails without running
    teardowns. The codex stays registered in the lab-host, the
    bare repo stays on disk. Two design directions: setup
    engines grab a release-on-rig-terminal handle that fires
    on any rig terminal-state, OR teardowns run unconditionally
    on rig failure (currently they only run when the upstream
    chain succeeded).

  - **Daemon ↔ CLI Scriptorium state drift.** `nsg codex remove`

    notified. Daemon's next guild.json write reverts the
    orphans. Vibers currently has three cosmetic orphan codexes
    (`verify-fixtures-{1267530e,2244ce82,40dd2663}`) that will

## Implementation discipline

  - **One commit per engine.** Each commit ships the engine +
    its tests + replaces the stub in `stubs.ts` + adds the real
    implementation file under `engines/` (or `src/probes/<name>/`
    for probes). Conclude the matching click in the same commit.
  - **Match framework conventions.** Each engine is an
    `EngineDesign` with `id`, `run(givens, context)`, and
    optional `retry`. Probes additionally provide `extract()`
    per the registry contract. Errors throw; the spider routes
    to engine failure.
  - **Givens carry the contract.** Document each engine's
    givens shape in a TS comment block at the top of the file.
    Manifest authors read those to know what to put in
    `ext.laboratory.config`.
  - **Use injected `_trial` context for trial-aware defaulting.**
    phases.ts populates it; engines read it for sensible
    name/path defaults. Codex and guild fixtures already
    establish the pattern.
  - **Use upstream duck-typing for cross-engine wiring.** No
    manifest-level "this is the codex / test guild" declarations
    — engines walk `context.upstream` looking for yields that

    discovery, and archive's probe-summary discovery all follow

  - **Atomicity is per-engine, not per-trial.** Don't try to

    forbids it — probes graft as separate engines ahead of

    the archive engine writes its index row atomically per-row;
    orphan probe rows from rig-failed trials are tolerated
    (analytical queries join from `lab-trial-archives` outward,
    so orphans don't surface).
  - **Live verification per engine.** After implementing, post
    a trial that exercises it and check the rig completes. The
    smoke-test pattern (write tmp manifest, `nsg lab-trial-post`,
    poll `nsg writ show --format json` until classification ===
    'terminal') works for one-off verification before the
    codified test lands.

## Pointers to read first

  - `packages/laboratory/README.md` — **canonical archive design
    spec.** Architecture, book schemas, atomicity, registry
    contract, CLI surface. The single source of truth for the
    archive subsystem.
  - `nsg click extract --id c-moma9llq` — full implementation
    click subtree.
  - `nsg click show c-momaa5o9` — archive design conclusion (the
    reasoning trail behind the README spec).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/phases.ts` — phase
    orchestrators that route into each engine; documents the
    `InjectedTrialContext` shape that every grafted work engine
    sees.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/codex-fixture.ts` — first
    real engine pair; pattern for fixture engines (validation,
    rollback, archive-presence safety check, `_trial`-driven
    defaulting).
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/guild-fixture.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out to nsg, with sequential
    plugin-install loop and duck-typed codex discovery from
    upstream.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/scenario-xguild.ts` —
    pattern for engines that shell out, parse JSON, optionally
    poll until classification-terminal.
  - `packages/laboratory/src/engines/stubs.ts` — what's still a
    stub. `lab.archive`, `lab.probe-stacks-dump`, and
    `lab.probe-git-range` are here. `lab.probe-trial-context`
    is greenfield (no stub yet — add it during probe work).
  - `.scratch/lab-trial-verify-*.yaml` — verify manifests used
    in live testing; useful templates for the codified smoke
    test.

## Live test environment

Vibers is the lab-host guild for development. After plugin code
changes, restart vibers' daemon to pick up new engine
registrations. The CLI tool (`nsg lab-trial-post`) reads source
per-invocation (fresh node process), but the running spider
loaded its engines at startup.

**Caveat:** vibers currently has three orphan codexes from the
previous live-verify session that survived a daemon write.
They won't run anything but force `--codex` selection on
`lab-trial-post` invocations until cleared via daemon restart.

For local upstream codex testing, `/workspace/nexus-mk2` is the
natural seed (the trial codex bare repo will be a per-trial
isolated copy at a base SHA). For plugin install in the test
guild, use `file:<absolute-path>` syntax (npm-compatible);
`link:` is pnpm-only and trips EUNSUPPORTEDPROTOCOL on the test
guilds (which default to npm).
